description_long: This object is a rectangular stone block with a distinctive pattern of evenly spaced, parallel grooves running across its top surface. The grooves reveal a contrast between a dark, almost black stone and lighter, marbled stone in the recessed areas, giving it a striking striped appearance. The block is solid and robust, with sharp, clean edges and a flat top, making it suitable for use as a barrier or obstacle in urban environments. The sides are smooth and unadorned, emphasizing the block's geometric simplicity and massiveness. The material appears to be a dense, polished stone, possibly granite or marble, with a high degree of surface hardness and a slightly glossy finish. There are no visible signs of weathering, engravings, or additional features, suggesting it is either new or well-maintained.
description: A solid rectangular stone block with parallel grooves on top, featuring a striking black and marbled striped pattern. The block is robust, smooth-sided, and has a slightly glossy, polished stone finish.
description_view_0: Front view showing the horizontal grooves and the striped pattern across the top surface.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth and regular spacing of the grooves, with the stripes running perpendicular to the viewer.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, displaying the same horizontal striped pattern and flat sides.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again emphasizing the vertical grooves and the alternating light and dark stripes.
attribute_location_context: urban sidewalk or plaza as a decorative barrier or seating element
attribute_installation_method: set on a leveled concrete or stone base
attribute_weathering_pattern: no visible weathering; surfaces appear clean and well-maintained
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: isolated or spaced as part of a row of similar blocks
